phrasalVerbs.definition: To make a copy of information on your computer.
examples: Make sure to back up your files regularly.
I back up all my photos to the cloud.
Have you backed up your work before updating the software?

phrasalVerbs.definition: To put information into a computer or other electronic machine using keys or a keyboard.
examples: Key in your password to access the system.
She keyed in the customer's details carefully.
Please key in the code shown on the screen.

phrasalVerbs.definition: To make a piece of equipment ready for use.
examples: It took me an hour to set up the new printer.
Can you help me set up my email account?
The technician will set up the network tomorrow.

phrasalVerbs.definition: To start or stop a machine, light, etc. working.
examples: Switch on the computer and wait for it to load.
Don't forget to switch off the lights before leaving.
She switched on the TV to watch the news.

phrasalVerbs.definition: To try using something such as a machine or product to find out whether it works correctly or is satisfactory.
examples: We need to test out the new software before launching it.
I'm testing out a new app for learning languages.
Let's test out the camera before the event.

fillInExercises.explanation: 'Switch off' means to turn off a machine or device.

fillInExercises.explanation: 'Back up' means to create a copy of your data.

fillInExercises.explanation: 'Set up' means to prepare equipment for use.

lines.speaker: Dan
lines.text: I just got a new laptop. Can you help me [set up|set up] the software?

explanationContent.whyCorrect: 'Set up' means to make equipment ready for use.
explanationContent.vocabularyFocus: *set up* — To make a piece of equipment ready for use.
explanationContent.sentenceBreakdown: I just got a new laptop. Can you help me set up the software?
explanationContent.commonMistake: Do not confuse this with other items from the lesson — here you need *set up* (*set up*).

wrongOptions.back up: *back up* (To make a copy of information on your computer) does not match the meaning needed in this line.
wrongOptions.key in: *key in* (To put information into a computer or other electronic machine using keys or a keyboard) does not match the meaning needed in this line.
wrongOptions.switch on/off: *switch on/off* (To start or stop a machine, light, etc) does not match the meaning needed in this line.
wrongOptions.test out: *test out* (To try using something such as a machine or product to find out whether it works correctly or is satisfactory) does not match the meaning needed in this line.

explanationContent.usefulTip: In conversation, *set up* is often used in informal spoken English.
